steps.title: Find the constant C using the initial measurement
steps.content: At $t = 0$ hours, $P = 12,000$. Substitute into the formula: $$12,000 = C(2)^{r \cdot 0} = C(2)^0 = C \cdot 1$$ Therefore, $C = 12,000$.

steps.title: Substitute the second measurement to set up an equation for r
Solve the exponential equation for r
steps.content: Since $2 = 2^1$, we have $(2)^{4r} = 2^1$. When the bases are equal, the exponents must be equal: $$4r = 1$$ $$r = \frac{1}{4}$$ That confirms B is correct.
